TextENGNew: ......Heh heh heh.
"Aaaahahahahaha...!!!"
"Oh, Keiichi-kun looks pleased with himself? ...Why!? How!?"
"Ahahaha! He's probably just awoken to the joys of cross-dressing~."
"Keiichi-kun, your headband is slipping. Let me fix that."
With clear amusement, Rena adjusted the tilted headband perched atop my head.
"So, Kei-chan — have you awoken to the pleasure of wearing girl's clothes in front of real girls~? Be honest now. It's alright! We won't laugh~!!"
"RAAAAAUGH!!!! You—!!! I'll MURDER you!!"
I chased her in circles for a bit, swinging Mion's bag around wildly.
My last-place finish had been settled early on.
Once she knew I was locked into last place, Mion went into high gear!!
She accumulated points with iron-fisted precision. By the end, we didn't even need to count to know who was on top.
After that, in the changing room...

Amid much wailing and gnashing of teeth, with the full force of humiliation and roughhousing brought to bear, Mion personally instructed me in how to put on everything from the headband down to the stockings.
...D-Damn you, Mion... what do you mean this will "definitely come in useful someday"...
"How is a man knowing how to put on an apron-dress EVER going to be useful in life!?"
"Shhhh!! Kei-chan, keep your voice down, keep it down! If you stay quiet, it might just pass as rehearsal for a school play~."
"Sh-She's right... If you cause a scene here... they'll... figure out you have, um... 'unique' interests."
UNGAAAaaaaaAAAAaaaaAAAAA!!
My better judgment strangled the scream before it could leave my throat, and I shuddered all over.
"You know, Kei-chan, your skin is really quite fine-textured. People must tell you that makeup goes on smoothly for you, no?"
Saying that, Mion gently stroked my cheek.
UwhoAA! No man gets praised for being easy to put makeup on!
"How does it feel to have on a maid's headband? It really is the finishing touch, isn't it! The headband is the soul of a maid costume~!"
"Wh-What do you mean, 'the finishing touch'!? Shut up! Stop messing with it!!"
"Hehehehe! Keiichi-kun is flustered. So kyu~te~...☆"
"That's right. Men really do love frilly things, don't they~. So — how does it feel to actually be wearing one yourself☆?"
"I d-d-don't feel anything—! Hey, stop! Don't flip it up!!"
"It fits snug, doesn't it. Can you feel that little draft around your legs as you walk~?"
S-Stop~...! Don't whisper things like that in my ear~!!
H-Hau...!
"You're turning red, you're turning red. ...Have you finally understood? That's right. Right now, Kei-chan is... exactly the kind of maid that makes every grown man's mouth water."
Stop it, Mion~!! Why do you have to whisper that right into my ear!!!
"Have you ever just wanted to hold a maid close to you...? Right now, Kei-chan, you've become one with that maid."
"Do you understand? That's a closeness that goes far beyond just touching. ...There... can't you hear it... that heartbeat... that breath...?"
"Mi-Mi-Mi-Mi-Mii-chan... that's... hau...!"
On the verge of complete overheating, a thin red line made its way down from Rena's nose.
"And after doing your makeup properly, putting on the straight long-haired wig you like so much..."
"...I'll coordinate you into the very image of your ideal girl, Kei-chan."
"And then, we'll walk through town together. All the men will only have eyes for you. ...Wouldn't that feel wonderful...?"
"Aside from one specific part of you, you'd actually be a real girl~~~!!"
Saying that, she tried to flip up my skirt!! Waaah!! What is she doing, the madwoman!! Stop it, not right now, not ever!!
*CRASH*!!!!!
When I came back to my senses, Rena had me in a headlock and was rubbing her cheek against mine.
"Keiichi-kun when you were squirming like that you were su-su-su-su-SUPER kyute...!! I-I-I'm going to take you hoooome~~~!!!"
Peering through the vice-like gap in Rena's embrace, I saw Mion lying flat on her back with her arms and legs spread wide.
...I see.
So this is what Satoko and Rika-chan feel like when they become Rena's prey...
After that, it took quite some time to calm Rena down from her frenzy...
"Later, Kei-chan. Today was the absolute best fun. ...hehehe!!"
"You'll regret this, Miooon~!!! I'll return this humiliation tenfold!! I'll win by a landslide and parade you around in something even more embarrassing!"
"GYAAAHAHAHAHAHA!! Only if you can beat me~!! I look forward to seeing you try!"
Mion tossed me my clothes, then left looking more pleased with herself than I'd ever seen.
...Right, then.
"...Now that Mion has released me, I can't waste a single second getting home."
"You're right. Rena isn't as good as Mii-chan at improvising. ...If we ran into anyone on the way, I wouldn't be able to cover for you, okay☆?"
After that, I sprinted at full speed!!
I was fairly certain I ran the 100 meters in around six seconds.
"Well, Keiichi-kun. See you tomorrow~!"
"Yeah! See you tomorrow!!!"
The fact that I made it home without running into a single person I knew must have been divine reward for a lifetime of good behavior!
Today was truly my lucky day...
Just then, the front door opened, as if it had been waiting for me.
"Oh, Keiichi. You're home. ...I was just about to go for a walk—"
...My father's reaction speed left me speechless.
...A normal person would have noticed something was wrong by around the "Oh, Keiichi" part...
"A-Ah... Dad. ...This is... you know that club I'm always talking about? This is one of our penalty games... It just so happened that I lost today..."
...My father rested a hand on my shoulder.
It was a strangely warm, slightly clammy hand...
"...Keiichi. Let's have a little talk in my studio."
"Of course, we'll keep this between us — away from your mother—!!!"
"N-No, Dad, let go!! There's a TV show I want to watch right now!!!"
Mion, you will rue this day!!!!
His artistic passion blazing at full intensity, my father dragged me off into the depths of the atelier...
"Keiichi, are you in? Oh, you were in the bath. ...Is that... toner?"
"Uh— n-no— wahahahahahaha!! ...M-More importantly, what's the matter?"
"Sorry. I have a bit of a headache and don't think I'll be able to make dinner tonight. I'm sorry to ask, but could you go out to eat with your father?"
Mom is a full-throttle superwoman at work, but she gets run down on a regular basis.
So this kind of thing wasn't especially rare.
"That's fine, but... what will you eat, Mom?"
"If it's just me, I can manage something. Off you go."
"Keiichi~!! We're taking the car, so hurry it up~!"
Dad was moving with unusual efficiency when getting the car ready.
He must have been hungry — he only moved with that kind of energy when it was time to eat.
"Sorry to keep you waiting. So, where are we eating?"
"...Hmm. You know, I found a nice little place recently. Let's go there!"
My father, who normally had complaints about everything, was actually praising something... surprising.
I wondered what kind of place would earn a high rating from him.
As one who pursues the way of the gourmand, I, Keiichi Maebara, was genuinely intrigued!
The headlights of the car sliced through the early darkness that fell quickly over Hinamizawa, heading straight for town.
I was wondering where this hidden gem of a restaurant could be... it turned out to be, of all things, a family restaurant right by the station.
"...Dad, when you said 'a nice place'... you meant this?"
A cartoonish logo in the style typical of family restaurants read: Angel Mort.
It had more the feel of a girl-oriented cake buffet than a proper restaurant.
"You've never been inside, have you, Keiichi?"
Come to think of it... I hadn't.
I'd eaten out when I traveled to faraway towns.
But coming back as far as Okinomiya station, there was no particular reason to dine out.
Home was only a short distance away, after all.
...I see. That was my blind spot.
We pulled into the restaurant's parking lot. ...It seemed to be doing well. Plenty of cars.
But the license plates were something.
...There were quite a few that couldn't possibly be local.
...Toyama and Nagoya, fine, but... Chiba?! ...Saitama?!
I opened the mental geography textbook in my head and calculated how many hours each would take via the major highways...
"Heh heh heh. It seems you've finally sensed just how remarkable this establishment is, Keiichi..."
"Wh-What do you mean?! People come from *that* far away just to eat at this restaurant?!"
I followed Dad up the steps and inside.
The bell on the door chimed as we entered, and a waitress appeared shortly to ask our party size and seating preference before showing us to our table.
Dad ordered two of the daily set meals.
They seemed to have something prepared already, because they didn't keep us waiting long.
The taste was... no offense to Dad, but not particularly different from any other family restaurant...
"...If we're just talking about the taste, it seems like a normal family restaurant."
"The taste is beside the point, Keiichi! Look~ ...It's great, isn't it~☆"
...I see.
Perhaps the food here really was beside the point.
The clientele was almost entirely young men.
...It called itself a "family restaurant," but there wasn't a single family in sight.
And yet it was packed.
The parking lot was full of cars bearing plates from all over the country.
...If they hadn't traveled from far and wide for the utterly average food, then there was only one other explanation.
Dad flagged down a nearby waitress.
"Excuse me~, the dessert that comes with the set still hasn't arrived~."
"Oh — I-I'm so terribly sorry..."
"There's a dessert that comes with daily set A. I've been waiting for a while now."
"I'm s-sorry, I'll bring it right away...!"
The apparently new waitress rushed off in a flustered hurry.
"...Dad. ...Could it be, this restaurant is..."
"Aren't they great~? The waitresses' uniforms here~~♪"
*WHAP*!!
changeReason: Original says 'BAM!!'; new says '*WHAP*!!' -- JPN スパーン suggests a slapping sound; WHAP is more accurate than BAM.

TextENGNew: "W-W-What are you doing, Keiichi?! My own father never hit me!"
"I'm your SON!!"
"Now listen, Keiichi — I never come here with impure intentions."
"Those good and cute and eccentric costumes — the impression they make! The artistic inspiration! I wanted you to feel it!!"
changeReason: Original uses French ('belle and attirante and excentrique'); JPN uses English loanwords (グッド, キュート, エキセントリック); new correctly uses English.

TextENGNew: "You must have been yearning for this kind of stimulation too!! Haven't you?! But coming alone is a little embarrassing. I know!! That's why I dragged you along! You can blame me for it!"
"Even if I take all the blame for it... I wanted you to understand this feeling, Keiichi!!!"
"...Aah... I need to use the bathroom..."
Launching into a stream of bizarre nonsense as a smokescreen whenever he gets embarrassed — it's my father's worst habit.
...I want to believe this particular quirk hasn't been passed down to me.
Still raving one-sidedly, he stood up from the table and headed for the washroom.
With him gone, I sat alone in the booth.
...Now that the moment had passed, embarrassment came creeping up on me at last.
...All I could do was sit there turning red.
The waitress from before came back, eyes flicking between her order slip and our table.
She approached my seat, stumbling over her words.
A more veteran waitress was whispering instructions from over her shoulder.
...Come on, just calm down. Say: "So sorry to have kept you waiting."
"Um... I'm so, so sorry to have kept you waiting..."
That's it, just like that! You've got this!
Whispering that into her ear, the veteran waitress slipped away.
This waitress was clearly still very new.
The way she moved — uncertain, nervous — was almost precarious to watch; it put me on edge just looking at her.
I sat quietly as she placed the dessert on the table with unpracticed hands.
...But still... as Dad said... what incredible uniforms these are...
Like a bunny girl and a maid costume and a frilly confection all blended together and divided by pi... genuinely suspicious attire. ...In any case, she looked nothing like a proper waitress.
changeReason: New preserves the JPN math joke about dividing by 円周率 (pi); original says 'all in a perfect ratio.'

TextENGNew: There was so much going on, glancing around... that is... you really didn't know where to look.
With this going around the restaurant... terrible for one's digestion it was...☆
Though the same seemed true for the new waitress herself, despite being the one wearing it.
The way she kept going red — it was surely from having so much skin exposed, more than from being new to the job...
Both she and I were embarrassed, averting our eyes from each other... a strangely platonic, mutually awkward feeling.
...I'm a customer, you know!?
I should be able to look all I want...
Just then, our eyes happened to meet.
We both blinked, staring at each other.
...Wait. Hang on. This person...
"...........Mion, is that you?"
Her hair was down, and she was so completely removed from her usual air of confidence... I hadn't recognized her at all.
It was Mion!!! What is she doing here?!
"You... What are you doing working at a place like this...?!"
"Uh, oh, um... I-I'm helping at my uncle's shop..."
Come to think of it... I had heard her say something about relatives running shops in town and her sometimes helping out as part-time work!
"...Ohhhh. That must be a lot of work."
The tension I'd been holding dissolved in an instant.
Once I knew it was Mion, there was nothing to be nervous about.
In fact, my animal instincts told me that the balance of power between us had flipped! That's right — I'm the customer!! My position is above hers!!!
And cutting across my mind came the memory of today's penalty game and its humiliations...
God does exist!
To grant me this opportunity for revenge!!!
Noticing the flames of vengeance kindling in my eyes, Mion shuddered and took a step back.
"Still... looking at you now, you actually suit this kind of outfit quite well, don't you~☆"
"I-It's embarrassing, so... please don't stare so much..."
I confirmed my opening strike had landed clean!
She is utterly defenseless right now!!
Confirming the hit, I immediately followed up with a combo!!
"You've got a pretty generous chest, Mion, it looks like it might spill out at any moment... I'm sure all the customers are staring~!"
"D-Don't... say... things like that..."
"Don't play coy~! I know you enjoy drawing all the male attention with that embarrassing outfit~! How else could you wear it like that~!"
"I-It's not like... I'm wearing this because I want to..."
Hang on, Keiichi Maebara. Aren't you getting carried away here?!
Even as revenge for that lingering afternoon humiliation... isn't this going too far?
Shut UP, Conscience!!
An opportunity to pay Mion back double doesn't come along every day!!!
"Walking around dressed like that — your mother would be heartbroken~!"
"*whimper*...!"
"That little almost-but-not-quite view is the best~! Come closer~!!"
"Eek~... Have mercy~...!"
"Your spirit of service toward customers is sorely lacking!!"
"P-Please spare me~...!"
"Hau~~~, I'm taking you hooome~~~♪"
"Not thaaAAaaat~...!"
Entering something like Rena's Kyute Mode, blood running from my nose in a torrent, I went all the way until I had no regrets left! I made good on my vow to exact full revenge for today's events.
"Alright! I'll let you off with that for today. ...Hau~~☆"
Mion's mental circuitry appeared to have completely shorted out from the embarrassment.

I thought perhaps I'd teased her a little too much — but a meek and flustered Mion was something you almost never got to see, so I called it worth it. ...Leaving aside what exactly was "worth it" about it.
"I had no idea, though. The unstoppable Mion, thrown off completely by being a waitress!"
"......*whimper*... Um, that's... not quite..."

"But what a surprising side of you~! I want to show the daytime Mion this! Actually, I want to show Rena and the others. Rena will definitely go into a frenzy of joy! Do you think she'd want to come work here too?"
"That's... that's wrong..."
"I never could have imagined in my wildest dreams that such a cute outfit would suit you so well, Mion!"
"I-I keep telling you, that's wrong!!"
Blushing bright red, Mion finally pushed out the words that had been lodged in her throat.
"What's wrong, then? ...What do you mean?"
"...I'm not Mion!!!"
...Huh? I was taken aback at those incoherent words.
"If you're not Mion... then who are you? You're Mion Sonozaki, aren't you?"
"Um...... I'm sorry. I should have said something sooner, but..."
"I'm Shion Sonozaki. ...Mion is my older sister."
...That completely out-of-nowhere claim, delivered so matter-of-factly, made my eyes go wide.
Mion herself seemed to doubt that such a far-fetched excuse could possibly work — she was red to her ears and fidgeting anxiously...
"Shion...? Shion Sonozaki?"
"Y-Yes... I'm often mistaken for my sister..."
"But when we made eye contact, your reaction wasn't the kind you'd have meeting someone for the first time."
"Th-That's because... my sister talks about you all the time... so I already knew who you were..."
That's a bit of a strained explanation...
...But as I watched her expression, pleading with me to believe her, the urge to keep teasing faded away completely.
For us, Mion as a leader had to always be someone brimming with confidence — a force that drew the rest of us forward.
To hold this moment up to the Mion of the daytime would surely damage something that shouldn't be damaged. That was the last thing I wanted.
So I understood. This little bit of foolishness here was a separate thing from the Mion of everyday life. That's why... she was "Shion."
"...........I see. In that case... sorry about earlier."
"N-No, as long as you understand. My sister does have a lot of enemies."
Well now, she's self-aware of that.
The laugh that escaped me was met with a laugh from Mion — no, Shion — in return.
"Is this your first time doing this kind of work, Shion? You didn't look very used to it."
"Actually... today is... well, Kei-chan is my first customer."
After saying it, she quickly added in a flustered rush that she hoped it was alright to call me the same thing her sister did.
...That completely transparent little maneuver was, for some reason, unbearably endearing, and I couldn't stop laughing.
"You mentioned working part-time at your uncle's shop. I thought you'd be more at ease with it."
"Oh, I have other relatives who run businesses. ...This uncle's shop is my first time helping here, so..."
I see — that makes sense. Anyone would feel uncertain at a job they weren't used to.
"Does Mion ever help out at this shop?"
"Oh, my sister is, you know. ...She's not great with things like this..."
...I know what she means.
"Makes sense. Mion suits the cool, composed type much better! So I think you coming to help here was absolutely the right call."
"...Huh?" Shion asked, clearly thinking she'd misheard.
"That uniform would never suit someone like Mion. ...But it really suits you, Shion."
"Huh, ......what do you...  um..."
Shion, just like Rena often does, grew starry-eyed and turned bright red.
"Wh-...what does that mean... exactly..."
...At least for this moment, it felt as though a person named Shion truly existed.
...Because the Mion I knew had no side like this.
Just then, I noticed the veteran waitress waving over from across the room.
"Sonozaki-sa~n, you can take your break now~!"
"Oh, o-okay...!"
Mion finally seemed to come back to herself. It appeared to be her break time.
"Well then... I'll head off. It was lovely talking to the famous Kei-chan."
"Sorry for teasing you after mistaking you for Mion. Tell Mion I'll have my proper revenge on her soon enough."
"Oh — that was the thing where they made you wear a maid outfit and walk you around outside, right~!"
That mischievous grin she gave as she said it was exactly the one I knew.
"That's right. Tell her that I intend to pay back every last drop of that humiliation!"
"Bleh~. More importantly, I'm going to tattle on you to my sister about everything today. Have her take revenge on you at club activities! Ahahahaha...!"
"Ahahahahaha!! Bring it on!! I'll take her down before she gets me!!"
Mion laughed with a soft, relieved smile, and disappeared into the back...
...I felt as though today had brought me something genuinely rare.
That's right — even the invincible Mion has things she's bad at, things that make her flustered and embarrassed.
And yet, of all the excuses — to come out with a twin sister.
I thought about asking Rena tomorrow if the sister really existed. ...But I decided against it. That would be unkind.
I'd decided that the person I had met here today was a twin sister named "Shion." So I should refrain from doing anything to deny that.
We hadn't made any kind of formal promise. ...But what had passed between us just now had become an unspoken rule between Mion and me.
A hand tapped me on the shoulder. It was Dad.
...That was a long washroom trip.
He probably came up with some new idea for an artwork while sitting there.
"Keiichi, ready to go? Let's head home."
We settled the bill, and Dad had his loyalty card stamped. ...It was almost full. The man's a regular...
"Was that new waitress just now someone you know, Keiichi?"
"Ah, so you were watching from the shadows..."
Dad and his peculiarly considerate timing...
"Someone from school? From Hinamizawa?"
"Yeah. She's the younger sister of my friend Mion. It sounds like she doesn't go to school in Hinamizawa — probably attends one in town. ...I've never run into her at school."
"Hm. So not all the kids from Hinamizawa go to the same school?"
"About half, apparently. The other half make the whole trek to school in town by bicycle."
As I talked it through, a growing sense that I really had been talking to Shion came over me.
What should I say to Mion tomorrow? I met your sister! ...Something like that, maybe.
Even in a situation like this, sharing a small secret with Mion left me feeling oddly happy and a little embarrassed.
"...Dad. ...Could this restaurant possibly be..."
"Aren't they great~? The uniforms here~~♪"
*CRASH*!!
"Wh-What are you doing, Keiichi?! Why are you suddenly grabbing my hands?! Father and son can't do this, it's forbidden... Wait, when did my son turn out like this~~~?!"
"I have absolutely no such inclinations!! I was simply expressing my sincere respect for you as your son!"
"Well, I'm glad to be respected, but what do you mean 'as your son'...? Hm? Are you harboring doubts about your birth? That you might be the fruit of your mother's infidelity...?"
If anything, I feel like that possibility is more likely on Dad's side... Wait, that's neither here nor there!
"Father, truly you are my father! Bringing me to a paradise like this on earth!"
"...Does that mean you are also...?"
"Heheh... No man exists who could stand before this and remain unmoved! No — no *person* exists!!"
Dad and I sat back and let ourselves openly leer at the sights, bold smiles plastered across our faces.
Like father, like son. Truth be told, I'm every bit the uniform aficionado my father is!
To satisfy the discerning eyes of the Maebara father-and-son pair, Angel Mort's uniforms are something to behold.

Like a bunny costume, a maid outfit, and a frilly dress all combined and divided by pi... truly a dubious ensemble. ...What I mean to say is these girls do not look like respectable waitresses at all.
What with all there was to see, everywhere you looked... that is... it was impossible to just stare.
Watching them walk around like that really is something. Terrible for the digestion, Dad shared, demonstrating his expertise by shamelessly drinking in every waitress in the room with his eyes.
"Don't get the wrong idea. This is research for my next exhibition."
He was using art as his excuse, but those eyes of his were suspicious. More than suspicious, really. That said, we were two of a kind here. We'd better keep this from Mom...
"Nevertheless, the waitress outfit truly is the quintessential uniform, one cannot yield on that."
"Indeed. ...Still, it's hard to give up the holy trinity: the sailor, the nurse, and the maid."
"All one wants is to gaze at leisure... and yet a meager scrap of conscience and a stunted shred of pride forbid it! That is precisely why we seek a paradise where such things are permitted!"
"...Don't you get to see those at school? Sailor uniforms, that is."
...That's kind of the point, you just roll with it.
"But! In the end, the classic waitress outfit remains father's personal favorite~~☆ And I will go so far as to declare that the defining detail is the stockings!"
"Th-The stockings...?"
"Despite the existence of a proper uniform, those among us who are shy and pure will find themselves unable to look directly at it — causing the eye to wander downward and land on the legs first."
"And then — the very first thing to meet one's gaze is the line and ornament of the stockings!"

"The more alluring those stockings, the more one is drawn to their smooth and modest curves — and gradually, like a seedling quietly unfurling from the earth, one slowly raises one's head, until at last, gathering one's full courage, one opens the heavenly gate and attains sublime power!!"
"In other words, it is precisely the existence of stockings that allows the waitress uniform and the maid dress to come fully into bloom!!!"
"Is... is that so...? Stockings carry that much meaning...!?"
"And yet, they do!!"
"Some waitresses with a poor sense of professionalism wear baggy socks — in my view, that is heresy! Apostasy! I will not stand for it!! They have no understanding of what beauty is!!!"

Dad had worked himself into such a fervor over waitresses that he was causing a scene. The other customers were glancing over with expressions of "what on earth is going on?" But Dad paid them no mind and kept right on talking.
"Keiichi — as a fellow enthusiast, have you a firm grasp on the history of the uniform?"
"H-History...? No, not really."
"I see. Ignorant still. Then I shall give you the introductory course — beginning with a lesson on the history of the sailor outfit!"

"Originally a military uniform of the British Royal Navy — 'sailor' being the very root of the word. The first to adopt it as a school uniform for girls in Japan was the principal of a girls' academy in Kyushu. However, it is said that at the time it was first used as a gym uniform rather than a daily uniform."

"But just the nuance of the words 'gym uniform' is something to savour. Design-wise it combined a sailor-style collar with a skirt, reportedly resembling modern girls' uniforms quite closely — however..."
Dad's impassioned lecture on uniforms went on and on. Even I had little choice but to sit and listen.
...The years of experience behind him are on another level entirely. My training is still far from complete...
"Father's inspiration is bubbling up irresistibly! No one can stop this creative surge~! Truly, the essence of uniform aesthetics lies in the stockings!!!"
"――Wait just one mooooment!!!"
*WHOOOOSH~~~~~*!! *THUD*!! *SKIDDDDD*!!
Spinning through the air with a grand rotation, executing a theatrical overaction worthy of a superhero film, someone touched down gracefully beside us...
...Who, exactly, is this?
"I do beg your pardon for this sudden intrusion. I have been moved, moments ago, to shed many burning tears upon overhearing your learned discourse..."
"However! In the name of Doctor Iri — I cannot in good conscience let those last words pass without a word!"

"Your starting point — that stockings are the foundation of uniform aesthetics — is, I grant, admirable. But to claim that this alone is the whole truth is to be far too shallow! Far too narrow-minded!!"
"To see the tree and miss the forest — you are no different from the monk of Ninna-ji Temple, who turned back at the outer gate of Iwashimizu Hachimangu and believed he had seen it all!!"
"Wh-What did you just say!?"

"Listen well!? The waitress uniform is a reinterpretation of the apron-dress — once, since time immemorial, a luxury exclusive to maids serving the highest classes — remodeled for the purpose of bringing comfort and solace to the common people!"
"It is a crystallization of the effort and love poured into it by countless predecessors, bought at the cost of their blood, sweat and tears!!"
"Therefore, to receive and fully savour the supreme bliss that those forebears fought so hard to secure for us — that is our duty! Our mission!! Our manifest destiny!!!"
...When exactly did 'destiny' become a synonym for 'doing as you please'? This man's thinking is entirely 'free.'
"Now! Open your eyes to this world of maids!! Celebrate and rejoice!! Burn into your eyes the happiness of being able to behold these stars that shine upon this earth—!"
"Ungh!!"
A satisfying thud rang out, and the man who had been orating so passionately stumbled face-first forward. Behind him stood a waitress with a tray raised above her head.
"You're disturbing the other customers — would you please keep it down!"
"Ah, ahaha... I'm sorry, I got a bit carried away..."
"...Having certain tastes is perfectly fine. Looking and enjoying yourself are within your rights as a customer. And indeed, part of our service."
"But if you're going to make an unconscionable racket, I will have to deal with you. Understood?"
"Unconscionable? I wouldn't say that. We've barely scratched the surface — haven't even gotten through two percent of the subject matter."
"I have a phone right here. Shall I dial 119 or 110?"
"Ah, ahaha... ...Either number would probably connect you to someone I know, so~."
"Then I'll call both. Makes things simpler♪"
"...Well, perhaps we can continue this another time. Farewell~."
The man gave a small bow and shuffled back to his table, crestfallen. Dad and I could do nothing but sit there slack-jawed.
Hang on... wait a moment...?
The waitress in front of me looks exactly like Mion! No — it *is* her!! But Mion in a frilly outfit like this — there's just no way!!
"Mion...? Is that you?"
"Huh? ...Ah, um..."
"It is you, Mion... isn't it?"
"...Ah. ...Could you be... Kei-chan?"
This girl who looked just like Mion stared at me blankly as she said my name. ...I mean, surely it's her?
"Come on... what do you mean, 'could you be'?"
"No, um... ...Well..."
She seemed to be struggling to find an answer. The girl dropped her eyes to the floor.
She really does look exactly like Mion, though. And yet there's something oddly hesitant about her responses...
...The look in her eyes has this reserved quality to it, as though she were meeting me for the first time.
What's going on? ...Is this Mion putting on an act? But why would she need to act...?
Is this a continuation of club activities? Some kind of scheme?? Is there a brutal penalty game waiting at the end of this!?

In any case, this is Mion Sonozaki. The deeper you think, the deeper you get — but as long as the conspiracy theory remains unrefuted, I can't let her see how rattled I am. Get a grip, Keiichi Maebara!
"Are you...  ...not Kei-chan?"
What kind of zen riddle is this.
Am I being asked to prove that I am, in fact, me??
Would "I think, therefore I am" count as the right answer here??
I couldn't read Mion's true intentions at all. I was at a complete loss for words.
"You're Kei-chan... ...aren't you?"
That's Mion for you... the moment she senses hesitation, she moves in without mercy.
"Y-Yeah, I am... But what are you doing here, Mion? And why are you... dressed like that?"
The outfit was already the kind that made it hard to find a safe place to look. Knowing it was Mion wearing it made it completely impossible. I found myself squirming and staring at the floor.
"Huh? Oh, ahaha! ...I'm sorry. I'm actually Mion's younger sister... Shion."
"What?"
"I'm Shion Sonozaki. ...Mion Sonozaki's younger sister."
Mion's younger sister, Shion? She has a sister...?
"Shion...? Shion Sonozaki?"
"Y-Yes... I'm often mistaken for my sister..."
"But then — how do you know my name?"
"Th-That's because... my sister talks about you all the time... so I already knew who you were..."
Now that she says it... they do look exactly alike, but there is something subtly different about the atmosphere around her... Or wait, could that still be an act...?
For now, I couldn't quite believe it outright, and I steeled myself to look at her closely... only to immediately look away. Pathetic...
"Mion mentioned having a part-time job — is that here?"
"Um... my sister works at a different place. ...We have other relatives who run businesses, you see."

Certainly, Mion strolling politely around in a frilly uniform waiting on customers would be... hard to picture. ...Ah, so could it be that she's pretending to be her own twin to cover for the fact that she works here...?
Well, come to think of it, I wouldn't mind seeing Mion in this kind of uniform... Fumbling an order, dropping a coffee cup, flushing pink with embarrassment...
"――Whoa!!"
Stop! This daydream is dangerous, Keiichi Maebara. Even as a fantasy, if Mion ever found out about it she'd leave you half-dead.
"...I see. So Mion doesn't work here..."
Hold on, hold on — "a little disappointed"? What is that thought doing in your head, Keiichi Maebara?!
"Hmm~~~, is this waitress a friend of yours, Keiichi?"
Dad, practically drooling, butted into the conversation.
"She's the younger sister of a friend of mine."
"Oh, hello~. I'm Keiichi's father. Thanks so much for always being good to my boy. I look forward to getting to know you both together~."
"It's lovely to meet you too☆"
"Mhm, mhm. Lovely to meet you too☆"
D-Dad has broken...
Just then, I noticed a veteran waitress waving over from the other side of the room.
"Sonozaki-sa~n, you can take your break now~!"
Saved at just the right moment. It would've been dangerous to let Shion stay near my dad any longer. I'd have been misunderstood by association.
"Okay! I have to be going."
"Awww~~~! You're leaving already~?"
Dad wriggled his clasped hands in front of his face, puckering his lips in an exaggerated pout. Even I, his own son, had given up trying to cover for him by now...
"Ahahaha. Kei-chan's father really is... how should I put it — full of personality."
"Oh, not at all~~~"
Don't be bashful, Dad. That wasn't a compliment. She's weirded out.
"It was lovely finally getting to talk to you today, Kei-chan. Well, take care now☆"
Shion gave us a bright smile and disappeared into the back of the shop.
"What a darling girl~~~~. The very image of my ideal waitress! Maybe I'll ask her to model for a sketch sometime~~~~☆"
"Don't!"
"Why do you care so much, Keiichi?"
"N-Not that I care, but... it's her sister, Mion, who'd care. And she's genuinely terrifying when she gets angry."
"Sister models — that's a possibility too. She looks just like Shion, right? Twin waitresses☆"
"I dare you to ask Mion to model. She will literally kill you!"
"Stop being such a killjoy and at least ask Shion-chan for me. You can do it when you see her at school tomorrow."
"Only Mion goes to the same school as me. I think Shion goes to school in town rather than in Hinamizawa? ...I've never seen her at school, so probably."
"Hm. So not all the kids from Hinamizawa go to the same school?"
"About half, apparently. The other half bike all the way into town for school."
Saying it out loud, something suddenly clicked.
changeReason: Original says 'the feeling that I was really talking to Shion came to me'; new says 'something suddenly clicked' -- JPN ふと気がついた means 'suddenly realized,' not recognition of Shion.

TextENGNew: Why, if they're sisters, are they going to different schools? And I'd never once heard Mion mention having a younger sister... could they really be the same person after all?!
Or are there some sort of circumstances? I could ask Mion when I see her at school tomorrow.
But if Shion is a sensitive topic for Mion... probing would be tactless.
Or maybe... she was pretending to be someone else precisely so she could say "I'll keep quiet about your father's embarrassing behavior" — giving me something to owe her...?
changeReason: Original and new present completely different interpretations of Mion/Shion's motive for pretending to be someone else.

TextENGNew: For now, I'd wait for Mion to bring it up herself before saying anything. Having made up my mind, I changed gears and set about enjoying the parfait together with Dad.
"...Dad. ...Could this restaurant possibly be..."
"Aren't they great~? The uniforms here~~♪"
*WHAP*!!
changeReason: Same as 131: 'BAM' vs '*WHAP*' for JPN スパーン.

TextENGNew: "Wh-What are you doing, Keiichi?! My own father never hit me!"
"I'm your SON!!"
"Now, listen, Keiichi — I've never come here with impure intentions."
"Those good and cute and eccentric costumes — the impression they make! The artistic inspiration! I wanted you to feel it!!"
changeReason: Same as 135: French vs English for JPN English loanwords.

TextENGNew: "You must have been yearning for this stimulation too!! Haven't you!? But coming alone is a little embarrassing. I know!! That's why I dragged you along! You can blame me for it!"
"Even if I take all the blame... I wanted you to understand this feeling, Keiichi!!!"
"...Aah... I need to use the bathroom..."
Launching into incomprehensible nonsense as a smokescreen whenever he's embarrassed — it's my father's worst habit.
...I want to believe this particular trait hasn't been passed down to me.
Still raving one-sidedly, he stood from the table and headed to the washroom.
With him gone, I sat alone in the booth.
...Now that some time had passed, embarrassment finally crept up on me too.
...All I could do was sit there turning red.
The waitress from before came back, eyes moving between her order slip and the table.
She came up to my table, stumbling over whatever she was trying to say.
A veteran waitress was whispering guidance from over her shoulder.
...Come on, stay calm. Just say: "So sorry to have kept you waiting."
"Ummm... So, so sorry to have kept you waiting..."
That's it, just like that! You're doing great!
Whispering that, the veteran waitress took her leave.
This waitress was clearly very new to the job.
The anxious way she carried herself was almost precarious to watch; it put me on edge just looking at her.
I watched quietly as she set the dessert on the table with unpracticed hands.
...But still... just as Dad said... what remarkable uniforms these are...
Like a bunny girl, a maid costume, and a frilly dress all blended together and divided by pi... genuinely suspicious attire. ...In any case, she looked nothing like a respectable waitress.
There was so much going on wherever the eye landed... that is... I really didn't know where to look.
With this walking around the restaurant... terrible for the digestion...☆
Though the same seemed to be true for the new waitress herself, the one actually wearing it.
This flustered state was surely down to the amount of skin on display rather than inexperience at the job...
Both she and I were embarrassed, turning our eyes away from each other... a strangely platonic, mutually awkward feeling.
...I'm a customer here!?
I should be able to look as freely as I like...
Just then, our eyes happened to meet.
We both blinked, staring at each other.
...Hang on. This person...
"...........Mion, ...is that you?"
Her hair was down, and she was so completely removed from her usual air of confident energy... I hadn't recognized her at all.
It was Mion!!! What is she doing here?!
"You... Why are you working somewhere like this?!"
"Uh, oh, um... I-I'm helping at my uncle's shop..."
Come to think of it... I had heard her say something about relatives running shops in town and her sometimes helping out part-time!
"...Oh~. That must be a lot of work."
The tension I'd been holding dissolved in an instant.
Knowing it was Mion, there was nothing to be nervous about.
In fact, my animal instinct told me the balance of power between us had reversed! That's right — I'm the customer!! My position is above hers!!
And cutting through my mind came the memory of today's penalty game and its humiliations...
God does exist!
To grant me this opportunity for revenge!!!
Sensing the flames of vengeance that had lit up in my eyes, Mion shuddered and took a step back.
"Still... looking at you now, that kind of outfit really does suit you quite well, doesn't it~☆"
"I-It's embarrassing, so... please don't look so much..."
I confirmed my opening strike had landed clean!
There's no doubt she's completely defenseless right now!!
Confirming the hit, I immediately followed up with the combo!!
"You've got quite a figure, Mion — it looks like it might spill out at any moment... I'm sure all the customers are staring~!"
"D-Don't... say that..."
"Don't play coy~! I know you enjoy drawing all the male attention in that embarrassing outfit~! There's no other way you'd wear it~!"
"I-It's not like... I'm wearing this because I want to..."
Hang on, Keiichi Maebara. Aren't you getting carried away here?!
Even as revenge for that lingering afternoon humiliation... isn't this going too far?
Shut UP, Conscience!!
The chance to pay Mion back double doesn't come along often!!!
"Walking around dressed like that — your mother would be heartbroken~!"
"*whimper*~...!"
"That little almost-but-not-quite view is the best~! Come closer~!!"
"Eek~... please have mercy~...!"
"Your spirit of service toward customers is sorely lacking!!"
"P-Please forgive me~...!"
"Hau~~~ I'm taking you hooome~~~♪"
"Not thaaaaAAAaaat~...!"
Entering something like Rena's Kyute Mode, blood running from my nose in a torrent, I went all the way until I had no regrets left! I made good on my vow to exact proper revenge for today's events.
"Alright! I'll let you off with that for today. ...Hau~~☆"
Mion's circuits appeared to have completely shorted out from the embarrassment.

I thought I might have pushed it a little too far — but such a meek and flustered Mion was something you almost never got to see, so I called it worth it. ...Leaving aside what "worth it" really meant.
"I had no idea. The unstoppable Mion, completely undone by being a waitress!"
"......*whimper*... Um... that's... not quite..."

"But what a surprising side of you~! I want to show the Mion of daytime this! Actually, I want to show Rena and the others. Rena will definitely go into a frenzy of joy! Do you think she'd say she wants to work here too?"
"That's... wrong..."
"I never could have imagined in my wildest dreams that such a cute outfit would suit you so well, Mion!"
"I-I keep telling you, that's wrong!!"
Bright red, Mion finally forced out the words that had been caught in her throat.
"What's wrong, then? ...What do you mean?"
"...I'm not Mion!!!"
...Huh? I was taken aback at those incoherent words.
"If you're not Mion... then who are you? You're Mion Sonozaki, aren't you?"
"Um...... I'm sorry. I should have said something sooner, but..."
"I'm Shion Sonozaki. ...Mion is my older sister."
...That out-of-nowhere claim, delivered so matter-of-factly, made my eyes go wide involuntarily.
Mion herself seemed to doubt that such a far-fetched excuse could possibly work — she was red to her ears and fidgeting nervously...
"Shion...? Shion Sonozaki?"
"Y-Yes... I'm often mistaken for my sister..."
"But when we made eye contact, your reaction wasn't what you'd expect from meeting someone for the first time."
"Th-That's because... my sister talks about you all the time... so I knew who you were..."
That's a bit of a strained excuse...
...But as I watched her expression, pleading with me to believe her, the urge to tease drained away completely.
I know that even Mion has a very girlish side to her deep down. It's just that she hasn't had much practice letting it show.
Like when I'd tried to give her that doll. Her face betrayed just how much she wanted it, yet she was desperately trying to act "manly" as usual.
...I wonder if she worries that acting girly would undermine her dignity as a leader?
So I understood. This foolishness here was something entirely separate from the Mion of everyday life. That's why... she was "Shion."
"...........I see. In that case... I'm sorry."
"N-No, as long as you understand. My sister does have a lot of enemies."
Well now, she's self-aware of that.
My involuntary laugh was met with a laugh from Mion — no, Shion — in return.
"Is this your first time doing this kind of work, Shion? You didn't seem very used to it."
"Actually, today is... well... Kei-chan is my first customer."
After saying it, she added in a flustered hurry that she hoped it was alright to call me what her sister did.
...That completely transparent little gesture was somehow irresistibly endearing, and I couldn't stop laughing.
"You mentioned working part-time at your uncle's. I thought you'd be more at ease with it."
"Oh, I have other relatives who run their own shops. ...So this uncle's shop is actually my first time helping here..."
I see — that makes sense. Anyone would feel uncertain at an unfamiliar job.
"Does Mion ever help out at this shop?"
"Oh, my sister is, you see... She's not great with things like this..."
...I know what she means.
"I think you might be wrong about that. The fact that you suit it so well is all the proof needed. I think you'd see a whole different and wonderful side of her."
"...Huh?" Shion asked, as if she'd misheard.
"That said... they say girls change completely depending on what they're wearing. I think Mion in this kind of outfit would be pretty cute too."
"Huh, ......what do you... um..."
Shion, just like Rena often does, grew vacant-eyed and turned bright red.
"Wh-... what does that mean... exactly..."
...At least for this moment, it felt as though a person named Shion truly existed.
...Because the Mion I knew had no such side to her.
Just then, I noticed the veteran waitress waving over from across the room.
"Sonozaki-sa~n, you can take your break now~!"
"Oh, o-okay...!"
Mion finally seemed to come back to herself. It appeared to be time for her break.
"Well then... I'll be going. It was lovely talking to the famous Kei-chan."
"Sorry for teasing you after mistaking you for Mion. Tell Mion I'll have my proper revenge soon."
"Oh — that was the thing where they made you wear a maid outfit and walk around outside, right~!"
That mischievous grin as she said it was exactly the one I knew so well.
"That's right. Tell her I intend to pay back every last drop of that humiliation!"
"Bleh~. More importantly, I'm going to tattle to my sister about all the teasing today. Have her get revenge on you at club activities! Ahahahaha~!"
"Ahahahahaha!! Bring it on!! I'll have her before she has me!!"
Mion laughed with a soft, relaxed smile, and disappeared into the back...
...Today felt like it had given me something genuinely rare.
Mion... or Shion, here — I never realized she could look so cute in a waitress uniform.
But of all the excuses to reach for — a twin sister.
I thought about asking Rena tomorrow whether the sister was real. ...But I decided against it. That would be mean.
I had decided that the person I met here today was a twin sister named "Shion." So I would refrain from doing anything to deny that.
We hadn't exchanged any promise. ...But that exchange had given birth to a quiet, unspoken rule between Mion and me.
A hand tapped me on the shoulder. It was Dad.
...That was a long washroom trip.
He probably hit on a new idea for an artwork while sitting there.
"Keiichi, ready? Let's head home."
We settled the bill, and Dad had his loyalty card stamped. ...It was almost completely full. This man is quite the regular...
"Was that new waitress just now someone you know, Keiichi?"
"Ah, so you were watching from the shadows..."
Dad and his peculiarly considerate timing...
"Someone from school? From Hinamizawa?"
"Yeah. She's the younger sister of my friend Mion. She doesn't go to school in Hinamizawa — she must go to one in town. ...I've never run into her at school."
"Hm. So not all the kids in Hinamizawa go to the same school?"
"About half, apparently. The other half make the whole trek into town by bicycle."
Saying it out loud like this, the feeling that I had truly been talking to Shion settled over me.
What should I say to Mion tomorrow? I met your little sister! ...Something like that, maybe.
Even with things as strange as they were with Mion, sharing a small secret between the two of us left me feeling oddly glad — and a little embarrassed.
